rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks only into the target implementation scope when bindings require subcomponent or port-level access.
Preserve AADL component structure by not introducing references to subcomponents or internal ports in type-level annexes.
generic_rules: Place component-type contracts on visible features only, not internal subcomponent paths.
Implementation-level annexes may bind outputs to subcomponent ports if the model permits it.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use only 'int', 'real', 'bool' as scalar types in AGREE declarations.
Boolean comparisons must match the port's declared type—do not encode event ports as numeric values.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type annexes.
Do not assume numeric semantics for AADL event ports without explicit data binding.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
